skill.name: ai-music
skill.description: Generate and edit music with RunComfy models through the runcomfy CLI. The skill routes requests to ElevenLabs Music, ACE Step, ACE Step 1.5, audio inpaint, or audio outpaint based on quality, budget, language, and edit type.
skill.summary: Create, extend, and repair AI music using RunComfy audio models.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, weak crypto, and reconnaissance matches. Review found no evidence of malware, prompt injection, weak cryptography, or reconnaissance intent; most command findings are documented runcomfy CLI examples. The remaining risk is medium because the skill authorizes a shell-scoped CLI, sends user prompts and audio references to RunComfy services, and documents token storage in the user configuration directory.

medium_findings.title: Scoped Shell Access to RunComfy CLI
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash access for runcomfy commands and provides runcomfy run examples. This is expected for the skill purpose, but it still executes an external CLI and depends on that CLI handling inputs safely.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration and command examples directly confirm CLI execution. The scope is limited to runcomfy, so the concern is operational rather than evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: User Content Sent to External Model API
medium_findings.description: The skill states that the CLI posts prompts, lyrics, tags, and audio references to the RunComfy Model API. This is core functionality, but users should treat submitted prompts, lyrics, and audio URLs as data shared with a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly describes HTTPS submission to RunComfy and an outbound endpoint allowlist. The behavior is intentional and documented, but it creates a real privacy boundary.

medium_findings.title: API Token Stored in User Configuration Directory
medium_findings.description: The skill documents that runcomfy login writes an API token to ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600. This is a normal credential storage pattern, but it requires correct local file permissions and careful handling in shared environments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path and file mode are explicitly documented. The stated 0600 mode lowers risk, but local credential storage remains sensitive.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography and reconnaissance matches occur in prose, pricing text, model guidance, and an exit-code table. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, system probing, or reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive Markdown, not code. I found no supporting context for actual crypto use or host reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Third-Party Audio URLs Are Treated as Untrusted Input
low_findings.description: The skill warns that audio URLs for inpaint and outpaint are untrusted and may influence generation. This is a valid semantic risk, but the skill includes mitigations to use only user-provided URLs and distrust divergent output.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly discusses untrusted source audio and indirect prompt injection risk. It is not a prompt injection attempt in the skill itself because the surrounding text is defensive guidance.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tool Permission for CLI Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill grants Bash access for runcomfy commands. The permission is narrowly scoped but should remain limited to the documented CLI.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The permission is visible in the skill front matter. Its wildcard applies only to runcomfy commands, which materially limits blast radius.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Network API Submission
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented workflow sends request bodies to the RunComfy Model API and downloads generated audio. This is expected but crosses a network boundary.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior and allowlisted domains are explicitly described. The pattern is legitimate for cloud generation, not hidden exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Credential File
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents a local token file under ~/.config/runcomfy. Credential files are sensitive even when documented and permissioned.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact credential path is documented in the file. The stated mode 0600 reduces exposure but does not remove credential handling risk.

content.value_statement: Music generation choices can be confusing when quality, cost, language, and editing needs differ. This skill selects the right RunComfy audio route and prepares the matching CLI command.
actual_capabilities: Routes new music requests to ElevenLabs Music, ACE Step, or ACE Step 1.5.
Chooses premium, multilingual, or cost-sensitive models from user intent.
Builds runcomfy run commands with JSON input for supported audio endpoints.
Supports time-range audio repair through ACE Step audio-inpaint.
Supports intro and outro extension through ACE Step audio-outpaint.
Documents cost, duration, and model-selection tradeoffs.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI and RunComfy authentication before use.
Sends prompts, lyrics, tags, and audio URLs to RunComfy services.
Does not verify lyric rights or copyright ownership.
Audio editing is limited to the ACE Step inpaint and outpaint endpoint shapes.

use_cases.title: Produce a Brand Jingle
use_cases.description: Generate a short polished vocal or instrumental track for a campaign using the premium ElevenLabs route.

use_cases.title: Draft Background Music at Scale
use_cases.description: Create many low-cost music beds by varying ACE Step tags and durations.

use_cases.title: Repair or Extend Existing Audio
use_cases.description: Replace a bad section or add an intro and outro with ACE Step edit endpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Instrumental
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a quick background track.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 60 second instrumental background track for a product demo. Use a calm electronic style and keep the cost low.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Vocal Song Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with genre, length, and lyrics.
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a 90 second indie pop song with female vocals. Include verse and chorus sections, and choose the best RunComfy model for budget and quality.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Multilingual Launch Track
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that needs language support and repeated variants.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create the same upbeat launch song in English, Spanish, Japanese, and French. Keep tags consistent and adapt the lyrics for each language.

prompt_templates.title: Repair and Extend a Track
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced editing request for inpaint and outpaint routing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this audio URL to replace the chorus from 45 to 65 seconds, then add a 20 second outro that matches the original style.

output_examples.input: I need a polished 10 second jingle for an ad.
output: Selects ElevenLabs Music because premium commercial polish matters.
Creates a compact prompt with style, mood, vocal choice, and duration.
Prepares a RunComfy command that writes the generated audio to the output folder.

output_examples.input: Make 50 affordable background tracks for videos.
output: Selects ACE Step base because batch cost is the main constraint.
Suggests varied tag sets for tempo, genre, instrumentation, and mood.
Uses short draft durations before longer final renders.
Selects ACE Step audio-inpaint because the edit is time bounded.
Asks for or uses the audio URL and start and end times.
Keeps tags close to the original style to preserve continuity.

best_practices: State the intended use, duration, vocal needs, language, and budget before choosing a route.
Use short drafts to test style before paying for longer premium renders.
Only submit audio URLs and lyrics that the user has rights to use.
anti_patterns: Do not send secrets, private lyrics, or unreleased audio unless sharing with RunComfy is acceptable.
Do not use premium generation for large exploratory batches without a budget reason.
Do not ask for unbounded audio edits when the endpoint requires start and end times.

faq.question: Does this skill generate music locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the RunComfy CLI to call hosted RunComfy audio models.

faq.question: Which model is best for premium vocals?
faq.answer: The skill routes premium commercial vocal requests to ElevenLabs Music.

faq.question: Which model is best for low-cost drafts?
faq.answer: The skill uses ACE Step base for cost-sensitive batches and background music drafts.

faq.question: Can it edit an existing track?
faq.answer: Yes. It can route time-range repairs to audio-inpaint and extensions to audio-outpaint.

faq.question: Does it handle multilingual songs?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends ACE Step 1.5 for broad multilingual vocal support.

faq.question: What should users review before running it?
faq.answer: Users should review CLI authentication, token handling, submitted lyrics, and any audio URLs shared with RunComfy.
